unit.note: full client Vitest suite (190 files); server unchanged (client-only terminal fix)

surface_verification.runner: playwright (chromium) vs isolated single-process Hono serving the built SPA + API (temp USERPROFILE, 127.0.0.1:3947)
surface_verification.justification: Real-browser proof against a live pty of the redraw-proof copy: (1) after term.clearSelection() simulates Claude's redraw wiping the live selection, Ctrl+C still copies the captured text to the clipboard (AC-1); (2) the mouse-only Copy pill appears on a settled selection and copies on click, then clears (AC-3); (3) a committing keystroke invalidates the cache so Ctrl+C reverts to SIGINT (\u0003) (AC-4). Ran green (3/3). This run also CAUGHT a real bug the unit tests missed — a bubble-phase keydown listener never fired because xterm stopPropagations handled keys; fixed by registering the invalidation listener capture-phase. Note: localhost is a secure context so the copy here takes the navigator.clipboard fast path; the execCommand fallback exercised in the user's real non-secure http/Tailscale context is unit-covered (lib/clipboard) and was validated live via console instrumentation.

behaviors.behavior: AC-1: Ctrl+C / Ctrl+Insert copies the last CAPTURED selection when the live xterm selection was cleared by an app redraw
behaviors.evidence: terminal-clipboard-handler.test.ts 'cached-selection fallback' block (empty live selection copies cache; Ctrl+Insert too) + e2e terminal-copy-cache.spec.ts test 1 (clearSelection then Ctrl+C copies) green

behaviors.behavior: AC-2: with an empty / whitespace cache the copy chord passes through as SIGINT (interrupt preserved)
behaviors.evidence: terminal-clipboard-handler.test.ts 'empty live + empty cache -> passthrough' and 'whitespace-only cache -> passthrough' + e2e test 3 asserts \u0003 sent after invalidation

behaviors.behavior: AC-3: a mouse-only Copy pill appears on a settled selection and copies it on click, then clears
behaviors.evidence: useTerminalClipboard.test.ts (captureSelection shows pill; onCopySelection copies via copyText then clears) + useTerminalSelection.test.ts (mouseup captures even when copy-on-selection OFF) + e2e test 2 (pill visible, click copies, pill hidden) green

behaviors.behavior: AC-4: cache + pill invalidated on a new selection gesture (mousedown, incl. cross-boundary origin) and on committing keyboard input, so a stale selection never hijacks a later Ctrl+C-as-SIGINT
behaviors.evidence: useTerminalSelection.test.ts invalidation block incl. the review Finding-A cross-boundary regression (OUTSIDE-origin mousedown clears the tracker) + capture-phase keydown-invalidate + e2e test 3 green

behaviors.behavior: AC-5: copy-on-selection auto-write stays opt-in / default-off; capturing-for-copy never writes to the OS clipboard (no #186 clobber)
behaviors.evidence: useTerminalSelection.test.ts 'captures ... even when copy-on-selection OFF (no clipboard write)' + existing EmbeddedTerminal.test.tsx copy-on-selection debounce/dedup block still green (351/351 terminal tests)

behaviors.behavior: AC-6: the copy write works with no navigator.clipboard (non-secure http) via the execCommand fallback
behaviors.evidence: lib/clipboard copyText execCommand fallback path (unit-covered; skips when navigator.clipboard absent) + review-confirmed the pill click retains user-activation for execCommand; validated live over http/Tailscale via console instrumentation (localhost E2E cannot exercise a non-secure context)
